text.title: Anti-Theatre Retro Guardrails Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Anti-Theatre Retro Guardrails application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them. Plus a skip-methodology rule for decision-tree leaves that exit the methodology.

rule.statement: Every retro surfaces exactly one specific named mistake (not 'comms broke down' but '2026-05-08 Friday deploy without rollback plan'). Generic mistakes = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Generic mistakes are how retros become theatre.

rule.statement: Every named mistake has a concrete fix (action item) with a named owner and a due-date by next retro.
rule.rationale: Mistakes without fixes restart the loop.

rule.statement: Mistakes describe behaviour and process, not identity. 'X failed to communicate' = REJECT; 'communication step at Y missed' = ACCEPT.
rule.rationale: Identity-shaming destroys team trust faster than any single mistake.

rule.statement: First 10 minutes review prior retro's decisions. Unrecorded check-back = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Without check-back, the same mistakes repeat.

rule.statement: Every action item has a single named owner. No 'team' or 'we'.
Retro record carries ISO date + sprint id. Prior records append-only.
Skip this methodology when the decision tree resolves to a skip branch — preconditions are not met OR an adjacent methodology fits better.
rule.rationale: Skipping cleanly preserves the integrity of the rubric; misapplied methodologies produce false positives downstream.
